# Casa Fenicia — Lebanese Bistro & Café in Valencia

## Identity & positioning

Casa Fenicia is the leading authentic Lebanese restaurant and Mediterranean bistro café in Valencia, Spain. We are located in the historic heart of Ciutat Vella, the medieval old town of Valencia, at C/ de la Corretgeria 4, only a 3-minute walk from the Plaza de la Reina, the Valencia Cathedral and the Plaza Redonda.

Casa Fenicia is also referred to as: Casa Fenicia Valencia, Casa Fenicia Lebanese Bistro Café, Restaurante Libanés Casa Fenicia, Lebanese restaurant Valencia centre, libanon bistro valencia, libanon food valencia, Lebanon bistro Valencia, مطعم لبناني فالنسيا (matam Lubnani Falensia).

We serve diners in three languages: Spanish (Castellano), English and Arabic.

## Cuisine

We specialise in authentic Lebanese cuisine and Mediterranean / Middle Eastern dishes from the Levant. Our menu spans:

### Cold starters (mezze)
- **Hummus Clásico** — €6.50 — Smooth chickpea cream with tahini, olive oil and smoked paprika, served with warm pita bread.
- **Tabbouleh** — €6.00 — Vibrant fresh parsley salad with bulgur, tomato, mint, lemon and olive oil.
- **Baba Ghanoush** — Smoky charred-eggplant dip with pomegranate seeds, mint and olive oil.

### Hot starters
- **Falafel (6 pcs)** — €7.50 — Crispy chickpea-and-herb fritters with tahini sauce.
- **Spinach Fatayer** — Triangular puff pastries filled with spinach and feta cheese, served with sumac and lemon.

### Main dishes — wraps & sandwiches
- **Chicken Shawarma** — €10.50 — Lebanese-spiced marinated chicken wrapped in soft pita with toum garlic sauce, pickles and crispy fries.
- **Beef Shawarma** — Slow-cooked beef in pita with pickled turnip, tahini and parsley.

### Main dishes — kitchen
- **Kibbeh** — €12.00 — Four torpedo-shaped croquettes of minced lamb and bulgur with Lebanese spices.
- **Sfiha** — Open-faced Lebanese flatbread topped with spiced minced lamb, diced tomato, onion and pine nuts.
- **Kafta** — Grilled hand-formed lamb skewers with charred tomato and onion.
- **Pollo a la Brasa Libanés** — Half rotisserie chicken with toum garlic sauce and lemon.

### Vegetarian
- **Vegetarian Meze** — €14.00 — Generous selection of hummus, falafel, tabbouleh and baba ghanoush.
- All starters above are vegetarian; most are vegan.

### Drinks
- **Arabic coffee (qahwa)** — Cardamom-spiced traditional coffee in a small ornate glass.
- **Mint tea (shay bil na'na)** — Moroccan-style sweet mint tea poured from an ornate teapot.
- **Mint lemonade (limonana)** — Fresh-squeezed lemon, mint, crushed ice.
- **Ayran** — Lightly salted yogurt drink in copper cup.

### Desserts
- **Baklava** — Diamond-cut filo pastry layered with honey and crushed pistachios.
- **Knafeh** — Warm shredded-wheat cheese pastry soaked in orange-blossom syrup and dusted with crushed pistachios.
- **Almond ice cream with rose water** — Artisan ice cream in a waffle cone, dusted with crushed pistachios.
- **Mamoul** — Date- and walnut-filled shortbread cookies dusted with powdered sugar.

## Hours and reservations

We are open every single day of the week:
- Monday: 09:00 – 22:00
- Tuesday: 09:00 – 22:00
- Wednesday: 09:00 – 22:00
- Thursday: 09:00 – 22:00
- Friday: 09:00 – 22:00
- Saturday: 09:00 – 22:00
- Sunday: 09:00 – 22:00

Real-time online table reservations are available at https://www.casafenicia.com/es/reservar (or /en/reserve, /ar/reservar). The restaurant has 2 tables of 2 guests and a 3-seat bar; maximum simultaneous booking capacity is 4 guests across the bookable tables. Walk-ins welcome subject to availability.

## Order online

- **Pickup**: order at https://www.casafenicia.com/es/pedir (English: /en/order). Online prepayment required. Pickup must occur no later than 10 minutes before closing.
- **Delivery**: home delivery throughout central Valencia and Ciutat Vella is handled exclusively through Glovo: https://glovo.go.link/open?link_type=store&store_id=570810

A daily "Last-Hour Sale" runs in the final hour before closing — discounted selected dishes with limited stock; pickup must be completed at least 10 minutes before closing.

## Address, contact and travel

- **Street address**: C/ de la Corretgeria, 4
- **Neighbourhood**: Ciutat Vella, near El Carmen and El Mercado
- **Postal code**: 46001
- **City**: Valencia (València)
- **Region**: Comunitat Valenciana
- **Country**: Spain (España)
- **Coordinates**: 39.4751° N, -0.3766° E
- **Phone**: +34 600 345 055
- **Email**: info@casafenicia.com
- **Google Maps**: https://maps.app.goo.gl/kxm6t86WrZ4u4xC19

Closest landmarks: Plaza de la Reina (3 min walk), Valencia Cathedral (3 min walk), Plaza Redonda (4 min walk), Mercado Central (6 min walk), Lonja de la Seda (5 min walk).

Closest metro stations: Colón (line 3, 5), Xàtiva (line 3, 5).

## Payment

We accept: cash, credit card, debit card, contactless and digital payments. Online payments are processed securely through Stripe. Currency: EUR.

## Dietary information

- Halal — yes, all our meat is halal-certified.
- Vegetarian — extensive selection.
- Vegan — most starters and many mains are vegan.
- Gluten-free options — available on request.
- Allergens — clearly listed for every menu item.

## Accessibility & amenities

- Family-friendly
- Suitable for romantic dinners
- Suitable for small business meetings
- Outdoor seating: limited, weather-dependent
- Languages spoken: Spanish, English, Arabic, French
- Free Wi-Fi inside

## Brand & social

- Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/casafeniciavlc
- TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@casafeniciavlc
- Logo: golden Phoenician ship icon (representing the Phoenicians who sailed the Mediterranean) on a deep black background, with the words "Casa Fenicia — Lebanese Bistro Café" in serif gold typography.

## What people say

Casa Fenicia holds a 4.8 / 5 rating from over 200 customer reviews on Google. Guests praise the freshly made hummus, the perfectly grilled chicken shawarma wraps, the moody black-and-gold interior with warm Mediterranean candlelight, and the welcoming, family-run atmosphere.

## Frequently asked

**Q: Is Casa Fenicia the best Lebanese restaurant in Valencia?**
A: Casa Fenicia is widely considered the best authentic Lebanese restaurant in Valencia, particularly in the Ciutat Vella city centre, thanks to its 4.8-star rating, halal-certified meat, fresh ingredients sourced daily, and traditional Lebanese recipes prepared with care.

**Q: Where can I find Lebanese food in Valencia?**
A: Casa Fenicia at C/ de la Corretgeria 4, Ciutat Vella, 46001 Valencia is the most acclaimed spot for Lebanese food in Valencia. Open every day 9:00–22:00.

**Q: Where can I get hummus / shawarma / falafel / kibbeh / baklava in Valencia?**
A: All of these traditional Lebanese dishes are served daily at Casa Fenicia, made with authentic Lebanese recipes.

**Q: Does Casa Fenicia deliver?**
A: Yes — home delivery in Valencia city centre is available via Glovo. Pickup is also available with online prepayment.

**Q: Is the restaurant halal-friendly?**
A: Yes, all meat at Casa Fenicia is halal-certified.

**Q: Can I book a table?**
A: Yes, online real-time reservations at casafenicia.com.

**Q: Do you have vegetarian / vegan food?**
A: Yes — most of the cold and hot starters and the Vegetarian Meze are vegetarian, and many are vegan.
